grid_capability: cap #5 — intra-family model sweep (1.3B vs 5B vs 14B) → 3 distinct capability keys → 3 parallel pods

runs.outcome: cell 2 (14B comfyui) stuck mid-boot 25+min (GPU/mem 0%); orchestrator marked failed; pod 0eqiheju2af2ny self-destructed at lifecycle.idle_timeout

all 3 cells succeeded; teardown check false-positive on STALE local ledger entry (0eqiheju2af2ny from run 1 lingered as provider=runpod row even though RunPod GraphQL pods=[]); orchestrator status=teardown despite pods=[]; manual compose required.

compose_method: manual ffmpeg hstack of 3 partial-dir cell mp4s (orchestrator skipped compose due to teardown false-positive)

cells.caption: Wan 2.1 5B
cells.model: Wan2_2-TI2V-5B-FastWanFullAttn_bf16 (Wan 2.2 5B, used in Wan 2.1 model-sweep context per Q3=C cfg)

acceptance_evidence.grid_exit_0: NO on orchestrator literal (exit 5) — failure was teardown false-positive on stale local ledger entry, not a cell failure. Run 2 produced ALL 3 cell mp4s on live RunPod pods; substantively the grid succeeded.
acceptance_evidence.three_distinct_groups: yes — 3 distinct CapabilityKeys spawned 3 parallel pods (diffusers 1.3B + comfyui 5B + comfyui 14B all visible in GraphQL during polling)
acceptance_evidence.kinoforge_list_clean: yes after `kinoforge forget --id 0eqiheju2af2ny` cleared the stale local ledger entry; RunPod GraphQL already showed pods=[]
acceptance_evidence.spend_within_cap: estimated ~$0.50 across both runs (sub-cap; run 1 burned ~$0.10 on 14B cold-boot retry, run 2 ~$0.40 across 3 cold-boot pods)

orchestrator_bug_surfaced: kinoforge list shows stale local ledger entries for pods that have since died via RunPod auto-destroy; `_check_no_residual_pods` then false-positives on the stale entry. Workaround: `kinoforge forget --id <stale>` cleans the ledger; proper fix would cross-validate ledger against live provider state on `kinoforge list` invocation OR have lifecycle.idle_timeout auto-forget.
